checks.describe: the slim KIND cluster is up and its node reports Ready via the isolated kubeconfig (Step 1). Self-seeds with make k8s-up if absent
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl get nodes >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate k8s-up >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl get nodes 2>/dev/null | grep -c ' Ready ' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 1 ]; then echo node-ready; else echo node-not-ready; fi'

checks.describe: the registry holds opsmate/model:1.0.0-candidate — the M6 loser the canary serves (Step 2, the M6/M7 seam). Self-seeds the registry + push via the M7 seed
checks.run: bash labs/m7/seed.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; T=$(curl -fsS --max-time 5 http://localhost:5100/v2/opsmate/model/tags/list 2>/dev/null); if echo "$T" | grep -q '1.0.0-candidate'; then echo candidate-present; else echo candidate-missing; fi

checks.describe: canary-server.yaml is the M8-shaped canary: its initContainer pulls the 1.0.0-candidate tag, and it carries the designed wrong-filename exhibit (the base filename in the -m arg, fixed in Step 2) with a clearly marked FIX line (Step 2)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/canary/canary-server.yaml; if grep -q 'opsmate/model:1.0.0-candidate' "$F" && grep -q 'initContainers' "$F" && grep -q 'FIX ME' "$F" && grep -q 'qwen3-0.6b-q8_0.gguf' "$F"; then echo exhibit-ok; else echo exhibit-bad; fi

checks.describe: both backends are 1/1 Running: base opsmate-model (1.0.0) and the canary opsmate-canary (1.0.0-candidate). Self-seeds: cluster up, kits present, apply base, apply canary with the -m filename FIXED to the candidate's, wait Ready (Step 2, the crash resolved)
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl get nodes >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate k8s-up >/dev/null 2>&1; bash labs/m7/seed.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; HIP=$(docker exec opsmate-control-plane getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}'); HIP=${HIP:-127.0.0.1}; sed "s#ip: \"127.0.0.1\"#ip: \"$HIP\"#" labs/opsmate/k8s/model-server.yaml | kubectl apply -f - >/dev/null 2>&1; sed -e 's#/model/models/gguf/qwen3-0.6b-q8_0.gguf#/model/models/gguf/opsmate-tuned-q8_0.gguf#' -e "s#ip: \"127.0.0.1\"#ip: \"$HIP\"#" labs/opsmate/k8s/canary/canary-server.yaml | kubectl apply -f - >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-model --timeout=240s >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-canary --timeout=240s >/dev/null 2>&1; B=$(kubectl get pods -l app=opsmate-model 2>/dev/null | grep -c '1/1 *Running'); C=$(kubectl get pods -l app=opsmate-canary 2>/dev/null | grep -c '1/1 *Running'); if [ "$B" -ge 1 ] && [ "$C" -ge 1 ]; then echo both-running; else echo not-both-running; fi

checks.describe: router.yaml carries the split: an nginx split_clients ConfigMap weighting canary, the X-Served-By observability header, and a small nginx Deployment (Step 3)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/canary/router.yaml; if grep -q 'split_clients' "$F" && grep -q 'canary' "$F" && grep -q 'X-Served-By' "$F" && grep -q 'nginx' "$F"; then echo router-shape-ok; else echo router-shape-bad; fi

checks.describe: the router pod is up and the split reaches BOTH backends: over enough requests through the router, X-Served-By names base AND canary (Step 3/4). Self-seeds the router apply + a background port-forward, drives requests, counts distinct backends seen
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl get deploy opsmate-router >/dev/null 2>&1 || kubectl apply -f labs/opsmate/k8s/canary/router.yaml >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-router --timeout=120s >/dev/null 2>&1; ( kubectl port-forward service/opsmate-router 18090:8080 >/dev/null 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/m9-router-pf.pid ); sleep 4; SAW=$(for i in $(seq 1 60); do curl -s -o /dev/null -D - --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:18090/health 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'x-served-by' | tr -d '\r'; done | awk '{print $2}' | sort -u | tr '\n' ' '); kill "$(cat /tmp/m9-router-pf.pid 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null; rm -f /tmp/m9-router-pf.pid; if echo "$SAW" | grep -q base && echo "$SAW" | grep -q canary; then echo split-reaches-both; else echo split-saw:"$SAW"; fi

checks.describe: a real completion is served THROUGH the router — the OpenAI /v1 contract holds and a usage block comes back, X-Served-By names whichever backend answered (Step 4). Self-seeds a background port-forward to the router
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-router --timeout=120s >/dev/null 2>&1; ( kubectl port-forward service/opsmate-router 18091:8080 >/dev/null 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/m9-c-pf.pid ); sleep 4; OUT=$(curl -s --max-time 90 http://127.0.0.1:18091/v1/chat/completions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"model":"qwen3-0.6b","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"ping"}]}' | jq -r 'if .usage.total_tokens then "router-chat-ok" else "router-chat-fail" end' 2>/dev/null); kill "$(cat /tmp/m9-c-pf.pid 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null; rm -f /tmp/m9-c-pf.pid; echo "$OUT"

checks.describe: the quality gate runs the golden generation questions against BOTH backends directly and prints a data-driven verdict (PROMOTE or ROLLBACK) — the module climax (Step 5). Self-seeds two background port-forwards; asserts the gate ran and reached a verdict, not a specific direction (candidate is the M6 loser so expect ROLLBACK, but the check tolerates either)
checks.run: export KUBECONFIG=labs/opsmate/k8s/kubeconfig; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-model --timeout=180s >/dev/null 2>&1; kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=opsmate-canary --timeout=180s >/dev/null 2>&1; ( kubectl port-forward service/opsmate-model 18092:8080 >/dev/null 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/m9-b.pid ); ( kubectl port-forward service/opsmate-canary 18093:8080 >/dev/null 2>&1 & echo $! > /tmp/m9-k.pid ); sleep 4; V=$(uv run labs/m9/canary_gate.py --base http://127.0.0.1:18092/v1 --canary http://127.0.0.1:18093/v1 --n 4 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo 'VERDICT: (PROMOTE|ROLLBACK)' | head -1); kill "$(cat /tmp/m9-b.pid 2>/dev/null)" "$(cat /tmp/m9-k.pid 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null; rm -f /tmp/m9-b.pid /tmp/m9-k.pid; if echo "$V" | grep -q 'VERDICT:'; then echo gate-verdict-produced; else echo gate-no-verdict; fi

checks.describe: the rollback is a documented, applied flow: the staged-promotion runbook exists with the 0% (kill the canary) and the 10/50/100 ladder, and it names the rollout-restart step a ConfigMap edit needs to take effect (Step 6)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/canary/promote-staged.yaml; if grep -q '0%' "$F" && grep -q '50%' "$F" && grep -q '100%' "$F" && grep -q 'rollout restart' "$F"; then echo rollback-flow-ok; else echo rollback-flow-bad; fi

checks.describe: the serving decision tree capstone artifact is present and walks all four patterns (Deployment, router+canary, KServe, llm-d) with when-each-pays (Step 7, stays after teardown)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/k8s/DECISION-TREE.md; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'Deployment' "$F" && grep -q 'canary' "$F" && grep -q 'KServe' "$F" && grep -q 'llm-d' "$F"; then echo tree-present; else echo tree-missing; fi
